info.name: MyRights Engine API (HackathonHashlama)
info.description:
Rule-based eligibility engine + POC auth + per-user right tracking exposed by Backend/index.ts.

Flow: a questionnaire submission (developer 1's form JSON) is evaluated by the engine and ranked rights are returned. A registration can carry a password (POC auth), and tracks its rights with a status the user manages.

Auth (POC):
- POST /login              - { email, password } -> { user_id } (matching registration id) or { user_id: null }

Evaluation:
- POST /evaluate           - stateless: questionnaire JSON -> ranked rights (no storage), confident matches only (> 55%)
- POST /evaluate/ui        - trimmed, presentation-ready evaluation for the frontend

Registration:
- POST /registrations      - store the submission AND its evaluation, returns { id, evaluation }
- POST /registrations/full - one-shot: store (with password) + evaluate + seed confident rights as 'in_process', returns ONLY { user_id }
- GET  /registrations/:id  - fetch a stored registration + its results

Per-user routes (user_id = a registration id):
- GET    /users/:userId/evaluation          - the user's stored eligibility, in the exact /evaluate/ui shape
- POST   /users/:userId/rights              - bulk upsert tracked rights ({ right_ids, status? }, status defaults to 'in_process')
- PUT    /users/:userId/rights/:rightId     - add/update the status for one (user, right) pair
- GET    /users/:userId/rights              - list a user's tracked rights (joined with right title/url)
- DELETE /users/:userId/rights/:rightId     - stop tracking a right

Status values: 'realized' | 'in_process' | 'worth_checking' (or null).

GET  /                     - health check

Set the `baseUrl` collection variable (default http://localhost:3000). 'Create registration (full)' saves the returned user_id into `registrationId`; 'Get user evaluation (UI)' saves the first right id into `rightId`. Run them top to bottom for a complete walkthrough.

request.description: POC auth. Body { email, password }. Returns { user_id } where user_id is the id of a registration whose email + (bcrypt) password match, or null if authentication fails. No tokens/sessions. Saves a non-null user_id into registrationId.

Stateless evaluation. Body is developer 1's questionnaire-result JSON. Returns { rights[], meta } (no profile) and only confident matches (percentage > 55).

Optional: add a top-level "overrides" object to supply attributes the form doesn't capture, e.g.
  "overrides": { "medical_disability_percentage": 60, "income_monthly": 4500 }
These clear themselves from meta.missing_inputs.

Trimmed, presentation-ready evaluation for the frontend. Returns { rights[], disclaimer }, confident matches only. Each right: { id, title, description[], confidence, source_url, steps[], missing_info[] }.

Stores the questionnaire submission in the registrations table (password is bcrypt-hashed), persists the engine results (registrations.results JSONB), and returns { id, created_at, evaluation }. The test script saves id into the registrationId collection variable.

One-shot onboarding for the frontend: stores the submission (password bcrypt-hashed), evaluates it, and seeds the confident matches (percentage > 55) as tracked rights with status 'worth_checking'. Responds with ONLY { user_id }. The frontend then fetches the rights via GET /users/:userId/evaluation. Saves user_id into registrationId.

The user's stored eligibility evaluation, projected to the exact /evaluate/ui shape ({ rights, disclaimer }), confident matches only. 404 if the registration id is unknown. Saves the first right id into rightId.
Fetch a stored registration row (form columns + results JSONB). Run a 'Create registration' request first to populate registrationId, or set it manually to a UUID.

Bulk add/update tracked rights for a user (registration). Body: { right_ids: number[], status?: 'realized'|'in_process'|'worth_checking'|null }. status defaults to 'worth_checking'. Atomic: an unknown right id fails the whole batch (404).
Add or update the tracked status for one (user, right) pair. Body: { status: 'realized'|'in_process'|'worth_checking'|null }. Returns the upserted row.
List the rights a user is tracking, joined with right name_he + source_url. Returns { rights: [...] } (empty array for an unknown user).
Stop tracking a right for a user. 204 on success, 404 if the (user, right) pair was not tracked.

Schedules an email reminder for a prosthesis. Body: { receiptDate: string, info: string }. The next email date is calculated as receiptDate + 3 years - 2 months, and inserted into the database. A daily cron job will pick this up and send real emails.

item.name: Toggle Step Flag for a Right

request.description: Mark a specific step for a specific right as completed or not completed. Body: { step: string, is_completed: boolean }
Fetch the user's evaluation. The 'steps' array inside each right will now contain { step: string, is_completed: boolean } reflecting the status saved via the Toggle Step route.
